openmeter/server/router.purpose: Pure v1 HTTP delegation layer implementing the generated api.ServerInterface: every Router method unpacks path/query params and terminates with a single typed handler call via .With(params).ServeHTTP(w, r). Owns no business logic — its constraint is that each method body ends with exactly one handler dispatch.

patterns.name: Handler delegation via .With().ServeHTTP()
patterns.description: Every Router method unpacks path/query params into a typed Params struct, calls the domain handler's named method (e.g. a.billingHandler.GetInvoice()), optionally chains .With(params), and ends with .ServeHTTP(w, r). No business logic lives here.

patterns.name: Config aggregates all domain services with nil validation
patterns.description: router.Config holds every domain service (~40 interface fields). NewRouter calls config.Validate() before constructing handlers; each required field has an explicit nil check that errors at startup, not at first request.
patterns.example: if c.NamespaceManager == nil { return errors.New("namespace manager is required") }

patterns.name: StaticNamespaceDecoder injected universally
patterns.description: All multi-tenant handlers receive namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder(config.NamespaceManager.GetDefaultNamespace()) as their first constructor argument so namespace resolution happens once at construction for self-hosted single-namespace deployments.

patterns.name: One domain file per resource group
patterns.description: Each resource area has its own file (billing.go, customer.go, meter.go, entitlement.go, etc.) holding only that domain's Router methods. router.go holds Config, Router, NewRouter, and init().
patterns.example: billing.go holds all /api/v1/billing/* methods; meter.go holds all /api/v1/meters/* methods

patterns.name: Stub unimplemented endpoints with 501
patterns.description: Methods not yet implemented return w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotImplemented) directly. noop.go anchors var unimplemented api.ServerInterface = api.Unimplemented{}.
patterns.example: func (a *Router) VoidInvoiceAction(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, invoiceId string) { w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotImplemented) }

patterns.name: Pre-handler body read for non-standard content only
patterns.description: Router methods read r.Body themselves only when the httpdriver cannot decode the content (e.g. raw Stripe webhook bytes), limiting via http.MaxBytesReader then passing via a Params struct.
patterns.example: r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, MaxBodyBytes); payload, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body); ... .With(AppStripeWebhookParams{AppID: appID, Payload: payload}).ServeHTTP(w, r)

patterns.name: Media-type dispatch on same route (QueryMeter only)
patterns.description: QueryMeter/QueryMeterPost use commonhttp.GetMediaType to pick JSON vs CSV handler variants on the same route; the parse error is Debug-logged (non-fatal, falls through to JSON). All other routes use a single handler.
patterns.example: mediatype, _ := commonhttp.GetMediaType(r); if mediatype == "text/csv" { a.meterHandler.QueryMeterCSV().With(handlerParams).ServeHTTP(w, r); return }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config (all injected services + feature flags), Router struct (all handler fields), NewRouter (validates and wires ~25 handlers), and init() registering CloudEvents body decoders (application/cloudevents+json, application/cloudevents-batch+json) for kin-openapi validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a service to Config requires: (1) a Config field, (2) a nil check in Config.Validate(), (3) a Router handler field, (4) construction in NewRouter. Missing any step causes a nil-pointer panic at first request. var _ api.ServerInterface = (*Router)(nil) catches missing methods.

key_file_guides.role: Declares var unimplemented api.ServerInterface = api.Unimplemented{} so the generated Unimplemented fallback stays valid. No real stubs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not remove — it anchors the compile-time check; if generated api.Unimplemented{} diverges after gen-api this line fails to compile.

key_file_guides.role: QueryPortalMeter is the only Router method with conditional auth: extracts the authenticated subject from ctx via authenticator.GetAuthenticatedSubject, injects it as a filter, and re-dispatches to QueryMeter. Other portal methods are pure delegation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The only Router method manipulating ctx with conditional logic — do not replicate; it should migrate to an httpdriver handler.

key_file_guides.role: Invoice progress actions (advance, approve, retry, snapshot-quantities) all use the same ProgressInvoice handler with a typed action constant argument instead of separate handlers.
Canonical pre-handler body reading: reads the raw Stripe webhook payload with MaxBytesReader(65536) before passing to appStripeHandler.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only follow MaxBytesReader + io.ReadAll + bytes-in-Params for new raw-byte webhooks; do not call io.ReadAll in any other Router method.

key_file_guides.role: Example domain file: each method is a thin .With(...).ServeHTTP delegation to addonHandler (e.g. GetAddon maps params into addonhttpdriver.GetAddonRequestParams).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep param mapping declarative — no defaulting logic beyond lo.FromPtrOr.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic, error handling, or DB calls in a Router method — logic belongs in the domain httpdriver package
Reading r.Body unless the httpdriver cannot decode it — let the handler's RequestDecoder own body parsing
Skipping the Config.Validate() nil check when adding a required service field
Creating a file here for non-HTTP concerns (middleware, auth, telemetry) — those belong in openmeter/server/server.go
Bypassing .With().ServeHTTP() with an inline http.Handler or w.WriteHeader/w.Write (except 501 stubs) — breaks uniform error encoding and OTel tracing

decisions.decision: Router is a pure delegation layer with no business logic
decisions.rationale: Keeps the generated api.ServerInterface boundary thin so the TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> oapi-codegen pipeline can re-run without touching logic; all logic lives in unit-testable httpdriver packages.

decisions.decision: Config aggregates every domain service and flag into a single validated struct
decisions.rationale: NewRouter is the single wiring point for ~25 handlers; nil-checking at construction surfaces missing dependencies before the server starts, making startup failures deterministic.

decisions.decision: One domain file per resource group mirrors the TypeSpec package split
decisions.rationale: Contributors locate any endpoint's delegation without grepping; file boundaries align with the aip/legacy TypeSpec organization.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 endpoint for a domain that already has a handler in Config
